title: Feed Discovery, URL Identity, Scope, and SSRF Boundary · Execution Trace: Feed Go Direct Vs Discovery
summary: Users can submit arbitrary URLs, discovery follows every alternate link, redirects change authority silently, and hostname checks occur only before DNS resolution. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go direct vs discovery increment in authorized feed discovery and network-scope policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Distinguish a direct feed URL from HTML autodiscovery and require an explicit bounded discovery policy.
Reproduce format, generated artifacts, migration validation, unit, integration, race, fuzz, vet, vulnerability, build, and smoke evidence from clean state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Direct Vs Discovery · Theory Feed Go Direct Vs Discovery
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 2940 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile bounded feed discovery, URL identity, SSRF decisions, and provenance; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one artifact, deployment, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile the release. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish a direct feed URL from HTML autodiscovery and require an explicit bounded discovery policy.

checks.description: The decision applies Feed Go Direct Vs Discovery to the changed stakeholder case.
